title: pheno-domain — canonical domain primitives (EntityId, Timestamp, Slug, Email, Money)
author: forge
summary: Authored the pheno-domain standalone crate at pheno-domain/ with five canonical domain primitives and a unified DomainError type. EntityId (Uuid), Timestamp (DateTime<Utc>), Slug (validated lowercase/hyphen/3-64 chars), Email (validated via regex), and Money (Decimal + 3-char currency, rounded to 2 dp). Each primitive implements Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug. Slug and Email also expose TryFrom<String> returning DomainError. DomainError is a thiserror enum with InvalidFormat, OutOfRange, and InvalidValue variants. The crate is registered as a workspace member so cargo test -p pheno-domain and cargo clippy -p pheno-domain --all-targets -- -D warnings work from the monorepo root. All 27 tests pass (including the 8 named canonical tests from the spec), and clippy is clean.

public_api.pheno_domain::DomainError: thiserror enum { InvalidFormat(String), OutOfRange(String), InvalidValue(String) } — canonical validation error for all domain primitives
public_api.pheno_domain::EntityId: newtype(Uuid) with Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, new_v4() constructor
public_api.pheno_domain::Timestamp: newtype(DateTime<Utc>) with Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, PartialOrd/Ord for ordering
public_api.pheno_domain::Slug: newtype(String) validated lowercase ASCII letters, digits, hyphens only; 3-64 chars inclusive; Display, FromStr, TryFrom<String>, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug
public_api.pheno_domain::Email: newtype(String) validated via loose RFC-ish regex; Display returns lowercase; FromStr, TryFrom<String>, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug
public_api.pheno_domain::Money: struct { amount: Decimal, currency: String } — amount rounded to 2 decimal places on construction, currency uppercased and must be exactly 3 chars; Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug

spec_alignment: The L4 #67 spec called for five canonical domain primitives (EntityId, Timestamp, Slug, Email, Money) with Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug; Slug and Email with TryFrom<String> returning DomainError; DomainError as thiserror enum with InvalidFormat, OutOfRange, InvalidValue; and >=8 named tests. All deliverables are present verbatim. The crate is registered in the root workspace Cargo.toml so cargo test -p pheno-domain and cargo clippy -p pheno-domain work as specified. The commit message matches the spec exactly.
spec_deviations: None. All spec deliverables are present and verified.
